title: 顺水推舟
summary:
Francis Claude, the daughter of the Earl of Trentham, married the lawyer Jeremy Claude after he used his shrewdness to settle her father's bankruptcy. While the marriage appeared successful and Francis played the role of a loyal wife and mother to their son, Anthony, the couple maintained a relationship characterized by reticence and a lack of verbal intimacy. Following the death of Anthony during his military service, Francis became more efficient and emotionally hardened.

While drinking coffee in a room overlooking their Georgian-style house's garden, Francis observed Jeremy exhibiting a characteristic habit of stroking his upper lip, a gesture indicating inner turmoil. Despite his initial denial, Jeremy eventually admitted that something was wrong, telling Francis that she had "made a bad deal." Francis initially suspected financial trouble, a concern rooted in her childhood experiences with her father's unpredictable economic status and the presence of bailiffs. She understood that for Jeremy, money represented security and identity, whereas she viewed it more cynically. As Jeremy appeared increasingly frail and sorrowful, Francis questioned whether the company was failing or if they would be forced to sell everything.
